name: rawpy (RAW Camera File Processing for Python)

performance.notes: LibRaw C library backend; fast raw decoding; postprocessing speed varies with parameters

agent_readiness.notes: RAW camera file processing via LibRaw. import rawpy. with rawpy.imread('photo.CR2') as raw: rgb = raw.postprocess(). PIL/numpy array output. raw.postprocess(use_camera_wb=True, half_size=False, no_auto_bright=True). Access raw data: raw.raw_image, raw.raw_image_visible. Thumbnail: raw.extract_thumb(). Camera models: Canon CR2/CR3, Nikon NEF, Sony ARW, Fujifilm RAF, DNG, etc. rawpy.enhance.noise_reduction(raw, h=1.5). numpy array output compatible with OpenCV/Pillow.

gotchas: Large memory usage for high-megapixel RAW files (50+ MB)
half_size=True recommended for quick preview vs full-resolution
White balance and color profile are complex — requires photography knowledge
LibRaw binary wheel; may need to build from source for unusual platforms
best_when: Photography workflows requiring Python-based RAW processing
Batch RAW conversion pipelines (CR2/NEF/ARW to JPEG/PNG)
Scientific imaging with RAW sensor data access
Machine learning datasets from RAW camera files
avoid_when: When professional color management is required (use Darktable/Lightroom APIs)
Video processing (rawpy is still images only)
summary: rawpy decodes RAW camera files (CR2, NEF, ARW, RAF, DNG) via LibRaw. rawpy.imread('photo.CR2').postprocess(use_camera_wb=True) returns numpy RGB array. Access raw sensor data via raw.raw_image. Extract thumbnail. MIT. Compatible with Pillow/OpenCV. Best for Python photography workflows and batch RAW conversion pipelines.
